content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings SSS and TTT. 

Find the length of the largest string KKK such that is is a subsequence of both SSS and TTT.


Input Format

First line of the input contains the string SSS.
Second line of the input contains the string TTT.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum size of the subsequence that is common in SSS and TTT.
constraints: S and T consist only of lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line is the string S, the second line is the string T. Both strings contain only lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the length of the longest common subsequence of S and T.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The longest common subsequence between "code" and "chef" is "ce", length 2.
No common characters, so LCS length is 0.
First case LCS length 2 ("ab"). Second case LCS length 5 ("chart").

solution.approach: We use the classic DP for LCS. Let dp[i][j] be the length of the LCS of the prefixes S[0..i-1] and T[0..j-1]. Transition: if S[i-1]==T[j-1] then dp[i][j]=dp[i-1][j-1]+1 else dp[i][j]=max(dp[i-1][j],dp[i][j-1]). Initialise first row and column with 0. The answer for a test case is dp[|S|][|T|]. Time O(|S|*|T|), space O(|S|*|T|) (can be reduced to O(min(|S|,|T|)) but full table fits the limits).
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S| * |T|) per test case, at most O(10^6) operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(|S| * |T|) integers, at most about 4 MB.
